name: libnet-pcap-perl
title: libnet-pcap-perl
summary: Perl binding to the LBL pcap packet capture library
description:
Perl binding to the LBL pcap packet capture library
Net::Pcap allows the user to interface with the LBL pcap(3) library.
Libpcap describes itself as "a system-independent interface for user-level
packet capture. Libpcap provides a portable framework for low-level network
monitoring." This modules gives a program the ability to capture data from a
network interface in a similar way tcpdump does.

commands.name: pcapinfo
commands.description: Prints detailed information about the network devices

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pcapinfo because Perl binding to the LBL pcap packet capture library.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pcapinfo as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pcapinfo` as documented upstream for pcapinfo.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pcapinfo with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pcapinfo --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libnet-pcap-perl (0.21-2). Perl binding to the LBL pcap packet capture library.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl binding to the LBL pcap packet capture library.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: pcapinfo - Prints detailed information about the network devices.
